meta.title: AAVF — Architecture Decided by Falsification
meta.description: Leviathan graph kernel ratified across 4 rounds × 9 gates × 4 candidates × real LLM. Hybrid IR survives every probe.

props.title: AAVF
props.subtitle: Architecture Adversarial Validation Framework
props.meta: Leviathan graph kernel · A2 × B3 × C3 × D2 × E1

props.content: Most architecture debates are unfalsifiable theology. We built a falsification battery — 9 adversarial gates, 4 candidate compositions, 3 reviewer agents per round — and let the graph kernel design ratify itself across 4 rounds. Reference (B3 hybrid IR) Pareto-dominated every round. Real LLM. Real costs. Real evidence.

props.label: The verdict
props.title: Reference Pareto-dominates
props.subtitle: Only candidate with verdict 'survives' across 9 gates

props.title: WINNER: reference
props.content: **A2 × B3 × C3 × D2 × E1** — 8 primitives, hybrid IR (RDF triples = NL ingress sugar, overlays = machine IR), two-layer kernel + projection, on_step admission, 5-state TruthState, locality default-on. Tied for highest attack survival (8.8). Highest UX feel (9.0). Highest bench (10.0). Only composition to honestly pass all 3 cross-IR patterns.

rows.value: 8 (entity, claim, evidence, proposal, gate, view, tick, intent)
rows.why: 9th primitive (Lease) didn't differentiate
B2 failed composition 3/10. B1 paid 2x reification tax. B3 honest across all 3 patterns
Single-graph attacker passed by being permissive, not correct
Richer than bool; D1 vs D2 still tied (round 5 work)
Approved 5-state; design-side enum drift reconciled
Round-1 finding #4 fixed: opt-in protection became default-on

props.label: Round trajectory
props.title: Each ratchet step tightened the verdict
props.subtitle: No round reversed prior progress. Evidence compounded.

items.title: Round 1 — TIE (INCONCLUSIVE-RUBRIC)
items.description: 4 parallel agents built falsification framework (~3500 LOC). Verdict TIE (margin 0.60). 3 reviewers caught 3 BLOCKER rubric bugs. 5 architectural facts established despite rubric noise.

items.title: Round 2 — SPLIT
items.description: Rubric fixed + 2 new candidates (overlay-only, triple-only-minimal) added to triangulate B-axis. Locality default-on landed. apply-overlay handler stub shipped. SPLIT verdict (margin 0.4).

items.title: Round 3 — WINNER reference
items.description: Round 3 rubric fixes (seed payload + Pareto check). InMemoryGraphAdapter add_link bug fixed (wiki: 0 → 8 link bullets). TruthState allowlist (REF drift 5.0 → 8.0). Reference Pareto-dominates.

items.title: Perf round — WINNER reference (real LLM)
items.description: Sqlite index added (Q P99 42.87ms → 0.72ms = 60x). OpenRouter classify replacing 200ms stub (claude-haiku-4.5). Bench gate added as 9th. Reference verdict = 'survives'.

props.label: Battery results
props.title: 9 gates × 4 candidates
props.subtitle: Final round with real OpenRouter classify

props.label: Evidence
props.title: 10 facts now empirically established
props.subtitle: Each cite-able to file:line. Each survives adversarial probing.

props.title: 1. B3 hybrid IR is justified
props.content: Only composition that honestly passes all 3 cross-IR patterns (triple, overlay, mixed). B2 fails 2/3. B1 fails triple+overlay. Hybrid is not redundant sugar.

props.title: 2. B1 reification 2x tax
props.content: TPM 2.80 hops vs REF 1.40 hops on standard query mix. Triple-only forces ~2x lookups for getEntity/lineage compared to hybrid IR's overlay shortcut.

props.title: 3. B1 closed RDF vocab rejects
props.content: 11 hardcoded predicates. Unknown predicate → reject at op-level. Cannot extend vocabulary at runtime without schema migration.

props.title: 4. C5 locality default-on works
props.content: Reference substrate coerces missing scope to 'global'. Round-1 finding fixed. Locality enforces by default, not opt-in.

props.title: 5. Permissiveness ≠ correctness
props.content: Composition gate measures whether substrate accepts ops. Attacker passes 3/3 by accepting everything. WINNER must pass capability matrix honestly.

props.title: 6. Strict falsification matcher
props.content: outcome === attack.expected required. Empty ops → INVALID, not vacuous PASS. Round-1 BLOCKER #1 closed.

props.title: 7. add_link wiki bug fixed
props.content: InMemoryGraphAdapter.apply() now handles add_link/add_claim/update_claim/remove_link. Wiki: 0 → 8 link bullets materialized.

props.title: 8. Sqlite index = 60x P99
props.content: Compound index (source_id, target_id) on links. Q P99: 42.87ms → 0.72ms. Cost: +1.6 MB storage, +12% ingest.

props.title: 9. Real LLM compositor latency
props.content: claude-haiku-4.5 via OpenRouter. 50 calls × 4 candidates. P50 781ms / P99 1782ms. Within 3000ms threshold. Cost ~$0.005/battery.

props.title: 10. B3 doublewrite cost = 3x
props.content: Every claim records as claim AND overlay AND tick. Amplification 3.00 measured. Acceptable for read-heavy workloads.

props.label: Real consumption
props.title: .lev folder IS the file-based graph
props.subtitle: Pentagon tests are the real-world use case. Expand them across these surfaces.

props.title: 🧪 Pentagon harness expansion
props.content: Already validates 11 adapters × 7 classes (now 9 with bench). Expand with more rtype facets, more fixture scales, more cross-IR patterns. Pentagon = production validation surface, not throwaway POC.

props.title: 📁 .lev folder
props.content: captures, decisions, designs, handoffs, plans, proposals, workstreams. Each markdown file is an entity. Frontmatter is claims. Cross-references are links. Already shaped like a graph; just needs ingest at scale via FileGraphAdapter.

props.title: 🔄 SDLC entities
props.content: Specs → claims about behavior. Tasks → entities with lifecycle. Pull requests → proposals with gates. Reviews → evidence. The whole development loop is already graph-shaped — just consume it through apply-overlay.

props.title: 🤝 Workstreams + handoffs
props.content: Every workstream.yaml is an intent (cascading behavior). Every handoff.md is a tick (one cycle). Every capture is evidence. Plug them into the compositor; the graph already exists in YAML.

props.title: 🔧 Real graph patches
props.content: NORTH_STAR principle: 'execution is a graph patch'. Replace ad-hoc edits with apply-overlay calls that compile to compositor 4-phase pipeline. Every conversation produces a patch; ratify it.

props.title: 📚 Docs as graph
props.content: docs/specs/, docs/design/, docs/adr/ — already entity+claim+evidence shaped. Frontmatter has lifecycle_state, last_reviewed, references. Ingest = one pass through FileGraphAdapter. Suddenly the dna ratchet has read-after-write replay.

props.label: Flowmind vision
props.title: decision-falsification.flow.yaml
props.subtitle: What if this all ran in the background?

props.content:
We just lived through 4 rounds of decision-falsification: pov synthesis → spec → parallel build → battery → reviewers → verdict → ratchet. ~3 hours of human-in-the-loop driving compresses to one overnight unattended run if we codify the loop as a flowmind.

The draft lives at `.lev/flows/decision-falsification.flow.yaml`. Knobs: question + axes + candidates + budget. Nodes: pov_synthesis → spec_generation → build_harness → run_battery → parallel_review → verdict_gate.

The wild part: every 'should we X?' debate becomes a falsification flow. Spec selection. Plugin choice. Refactor proposals. Onboarding paths. The graph stops being aspirational and starts being a measured optimum.

props.caption: The dream-flow loop — ratchet only tightens
props.content:
graph TD
  A[question + axes + candidates + budget] --> B[pov_synthesis]
  B --> C[spec_generation]
  C --> D[build_harness]
  D --> E[run_battery]
  E --> F[parallel_review]
  F --> G{verdict_gate}
  G -->|WINNER| H[ratify into dna/graph.yaml]
  G -->|INCONCLUSIVE_RUBRIC| I[fix_rubric]
  G -->|SPLIT| J[narrow_axes]
  G -->|TIE_EQUIVALENT| K[emit_equivalence]
  I --> D
  J --> D
  H --> L[evidence trail provenance.jsonl]
  K --> L
  L --> M[end]

props.title: 5-step consumption sequence
props.content:
**1. Wire apply-overlay into discourse-graph plugin** (~1 day, smallest wedge).
**2. Ship ViewEngine v0** (~1 week, biggest visible win).
**3. Start provenance.jsonl collection in production** (~2 days, training corpus).
**4. Implement parametric build_candidates_parallel** (~3 days, unblocks AAVF flow runtime).
**5. Wire AAVF flow into AgentPing** (~2 days, 'queue decision' surface).

Total: ~3 weeks to close the consumption gap.

Pentagon tests ARE the real usage. The .lev folder IS the file-based graph waiting for consumption. Every captured workstream, every handoff, every spec — the graph already exists. We just haven't ingested it yet.
